name: Tether
description:
Tether creates persistent visual connections between tokens on the Roll20 VTT. Connect two tokens with a dynamic PathV2 line that follows them as they move, and optionally reacts as they drift apart.

KEY FEATURES:
• Connect two selected tokens with a dynamic PathV2 line
• Automatically redraws the connection when either token moves
• Supports multiple simultaneous tethers
• Persists across API restarts, with retry-based reconciliation so momentary sandbox load delays never wipe out valid tethers
• Automatically removes a tether the instant either token is deleted, and prunes any that went stale while the sandbox was offline
• Update existing tethers by running the command again with new options
• Customize width, color, layer, and Dynamic Lighting barrier type
• Optionally set a distance threshold and choose how the tether reacts as tokens approach or exceed it: fade out, auto-remove, change color, or scale opacity/width smoothly with distance
• Threshold distance is measured using the page's own grid scale and diagonal-counting rule (D&D 5E, Pathfinder/3.5E, Manhattan, or Euclidean), so it stays accurate across differently configured maps
• Includes a debug command to preview the exact computed distance and effective color/width before committing to settings

USAGE:
1. Select exactly two tokens
2. Run !tether to create a connection
3. Move either token and the tether updates automatically
4. Select the same two tokens and run !untether to remove the connection

BASIC OPTIONS:
!tether width|5 color|0000ff layer|objects type|transparent

• width - Line thickness (default: 5)
• color - Hexadecimal value, with or without a leading #. An optional two-digit alpha suffix is supported (e.g. color|00ff00cc) and is used as the tether's base opacity when threshold/exceeds are in play.
• layer - objects, gmlayer, map, walls, foreground (default: objects)
• type - transparent, wall, or oneWay Dynamic Lighting barrier (default: transparent)

RANGE OPTIONS:
!tether threshold|60 exceeds|attenuate

• threshold - Maximum distance for the tether, in the page's own scale units (e.g. feet). If omitted, the tether always renders normally regardless of distance.
• exceeds - Controls what happens once threshold is passed. Only relevant when threshold is also set:
   - off (default): line becomes fully transparent past the threshold but the tether stays active; returns to normal the moment tokens are back in range
   - delete: the tether is automatically removed, as if !untether were run, the instant the threshold is exceeded
   - a hex color: the line switches to this color past the threshold, reverting when back in range
   - attenuate: opacity scales smoothly with distance - full opacity within 5 units, fading to 20% opacity as tokens approach the threshold, then fully transparent once exceeded; reverses smoothly as tokens close the distance again
   - stretch: same distance-based scaling as attenuate, but affects line width instead of opacity - full width when close, tapering to a width of 1 at the threshold, then staying at width 1 (fully transparent) once exceeded

DEBUG:
!tether-debug
!tether-debug threshold|60 exceeds|attenuate color|00ff00

Select exactly two tokens and run the command to whisper the computed distance and, if threshold/exceeds are included, a preview of the effective color/width - without creating a tether. Useful for confirming grid math on a page before committing to a threshold value.

REMOVING TETHERS:
!untether - remove the tether between exactly two selected tokens
!untether selected - remove every tether involving any of the selected tokens
!untether all - remove every tether on the current page

EXAMPLES:
!tether
!tether width|10 color|00ff00
!tether layer|walls type|wall color|ffffff width|5
!tether threshold|30 exceeds|delete color|ff0000
!tether threshold|60 exceeds|attenuate color|00aaff width|4
!tether threshold|40 exceeds|stretch color|ffcc00 width|8

PERFECT FOR:
Magical connections, spell effects, chains and restraints, leashes with a maximum range, creature bonds that weaken with distance, proximity-based traps or wards (exceeds|delete), moveable dynamic lighting barriers, and any other situation where two objects need to remain visually linked, with or without a range limit.
